prompt: You've just implemented a large authentication overhaul touching 18 files across three directories: auth/ (7 files), middleware/ (6 files), and tests/ (5 files). Walk me through Phase 6 quality review using the feature-dev workflow — specifically explain how many code-simplifier agents you launch, how you partition the files, and what you do to prevent conflicts.
assertions: Identifies this as a large change (18 files, >5) and scales to multiple code-simplifier agents rather than a single agent.
Partitions the 18 files into non-overlapping groups — e.g., one agent per directory (auth/, middleware/, tests/) — so each file goes to exactly one agent.
Explicitly states that no file may appear in more than one agent's scope to prevent conflicting edits.
Launches the multiple code-simplifier agents in parallel (not sequentially).
Still launches code-reviewer agents as a separate step after the code-simplifier pass.
